title: SQL keywords should be delimited by whitespace
description: SQL keywords should be delimited by whitespaceAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugsqlWhy is this an issue?When concatenating strings, it is very easy to forget a whitespace.In some scenarios this might cause runtime errors, one of which is while creating an SQL query via concatenation:string select = "SELECT p.FirstName, p.LastName, p.PhoneNumber" + "FROM Person as p" + // Noncompliant: concatenation results in "p.PhoneNumberFROM" "WHERE p.Id = @Id"; // Noncompliant: concatenation results in "pWHERE"This rule raises an issue when the spacing around SQL keywords appears to be missing, making the concatenated string invalid SQL syntax. It would require the user to add the appropriate whitespaces:string select = "SELECT p.FirstName, p.LastName, p.PhoneNumber" + " FROM Person
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
string select = "SELECT p.FirstName, p.LastName, p.PhoneNumber" +
        "FROM Person as p" +    // Noncompliant: concatenation results in "p.PhoneNumberFROM"
        "WHERE p.Id = @Id";     // Noncompliant: concatenation results in "pWHERE"

checks.message: SQL keywords should be delimited by whitespace in concatenated strings.

never_trigger_on: Properly formatted SQL with leading whitespace
logic.reasoning: Missing whitespace in SQL concatenation causes syntax errors like 'columnFROM' instead of 'column FROM'.
